destinations.evidence: Encrypted-output fee computation falls back to the plaintext envelope when encrypted pricing decode fails, then maps plaintext has_error responses to the constant LLM error fee.

equivalence_argument: For encrypted-output jobs, plaintext has_error responses are not silently dropped: after encrypted pricing decode fails, the same plaintext has_error prefix path reaches compute_llm_executor_fee and returns the constant fee.
notes: Malformed plaintext non-error responses still fail; only explicit has_error responses use the constant fee.
rationale: The existing has_error handling returns a prefixed error that compute_llm_executor_fee maps to the constant LLM error fee; this commit makes that path reachable for plaintext error responses returned during encrypted-output jobs. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If the fallback non-encrypted LLM response has has_error=true, fee computation treats it as an executor error and applies LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI so the transaction can settle on-chain instead of being silently dropped.
suggested_falsification_tests: Pass compute_llm_executor_fee an encrypted-output job result containing a plaintext non-encrypted response with has_error=true and an error message; assert the returned fee is LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI and fallback_reason contains has_error.
summary: Plaintext fallback LLM errors settle with the constant fee.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Pass compute_llm_executor_fee an encrypted-output result containing a plaintext has_error=true envelope and assert it returns LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI.
title: Plaintext LLM errors settle with constant fee
